question: Based on Table 1.3-1 and Table 5.1-2, is the NuScale thermal efficiency consistent between the overview and detailed data?

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams, legends, and tables used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, considering criteria such as maximizing electrical generation and maintaining safety margins. Key parameters like coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties addressed through testing data and design requirements. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties related to steam generator tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each guide is applicable, not applicable, conforms, or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Specifically, RG 8.15 is noted as not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, core design, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses smaller, simpler, and more integrated components compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0114.title: Figure 1.7-3c: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0114.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and defines the standard symbols for various equipment such as tanks, vessels, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, filters, HVAC components, and other related devices, ensuring consistent interpretation of the diagrams throughout the documentation.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing standard symbols and abbreviations for valve positions, actuator types, and operational statuses. It explains the meaning of terms such as normally open/closed, fail positions, and various actuator mechanisms to ensure consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integrated into the RPV.

nuscale_ch01_0685.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.7 AssumptionsInitial Core Flow
nuscale_ch01_0685.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures inside and outside containment, specifically addressing assumptions such as initial core flow, core burnup, and postulated failures of main steam lines. It notes that NuScale uses critical heat flux as a more accurate descriptor of plant phenomena than departure from nucleate boiling, and clarifies that operator action is not required to mitigate steam line break consequences. Additionally, it states that for radiological consequences, SRP Section 15.0.3 supersedes the methodologies in SRP 15.1.5, while nonradiological analysis provisions remain applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0738.title: SRP 15.4.3, Rev 3: Control Rod Misoperation (System Malfunction or Operator Error)II.1 Thermal Margin Limits
nuscale_ch01_0738.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for control rod misoperation events, focusing on thermal margin limits, fuel centerline temperatures, and uniform cladding strain. NuScale applies appropriate safety limits and methodologies, such as using CHF instead of DNBR and the 95/95 MCHFR approach, to ensure fuel integrity and prevent failures. Certain criteria are noted as not applicable due to differences in reactor design compared to traditional PWRs and BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0737.title: SRP 15.4.2, Rev 3: Uncontrolled Control Rod Assembly Withdrawal at PowerII.1.C Uniform Cladding Strain
nuscale_ch01_0737.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various accident scenarios involving control rod assembly withdrawal and misoperation. It notes that certain criteria, such as uniform cladding strain, are not applicable to NuScale due to differences from BWR and PWR designs, while other criteria are met through specific safety limits and methodologies (e.g., heat generation rate limits and the 95/95 MCHFR approach). Additionally, guidance for some events is addressed in other sections due to the unique features of the NuScale reactor design.

nuscale_ch01_0030.title: Fuel Handling and Storage Systems
nuscale_ch01_0030.summary: The fuel handling and storage systems are located in the west end of the Reactor Building (RXB) and include designated areas for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock. These facilities support safe fuel management and reactor maintenance activities within the plant.

nuscale_ch01_0029.title: Power Conversion System
nuscale_ch01_0029.summary: The Power Conversion System in the NuScale plant includes a main steam system, turbine generator, condenser, cooling tower, and condensate/feedwater system, allowing flexible operation of multiple modules for grid demand and maintenance. Supporting systems include reactor component cooling, pool cooling, and circulating water systems, which manage heat removal from various plant components and ensure efficient plant operation.

nuscale_ch01_0776.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.1 Evaluation of ECCS Performance
nuscale_ch01_0776.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCAs) and related radiological consequences. It notes that while some guidance and technical specifications are applicable, certain radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by more current regulatory sections. Additionally, the NuScale design justifies exemptions from specific requirements, incorporating only technically relevant aspects into its safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0774.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) Last two sentences of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Plant-Specific Technical Specifications for Primary and Secondary Coolant System Iodine Activity
nuscale_ch01_0774.summary: The section explains that while the guidance on technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity is applicable to the NuScale design, the radiological acceptance criteria are instead governed by SRP Section 15.0.3, which supersedes the original criteria. Additionally, certain sections and criteria are noted as not applicable to the NuScale design, particularly those relevant only to BWRs or superseded by other regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section B discusses the establishment of steady state values for system performance, which would be reflected in the detailed drawings and figures referenced in Section A, indicating that the overview and detailed data should be consistent regarding thermal efficiency.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify design information, which can support the regulatory conformance claims made in Section B regarding radiation protection design features.

Section A references the use of figures for visual clarification of design information, while Section B provides the specific legend used in those figures, connecting the general description in A to the detailed symbols in B.

Section A introduces the use of figures (such as P&IDs) for visual clarification of design information, while Section B provides the specific legend necessary to interpret those P&ID figures, thus Section B specifies the meaning of symbols referenced in Section A.

Section B (Table 1.3-1) provides nominal thermal efficiency values for NuScale in an overview context, while Section A (Table 5.1-2) establishes detailed steady-state performance parameters, allowing a direct comparison to determine if the detailed data is consistent with the overview values.

Section A discusses the optimization of system performance and steady state parameters relevant to thermal efficiency, while Section B provides legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are necessary for understanding the detailed system configuration referenced in Section A.

Section A and Section B both provide legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, with Section A focusing on tanks, vessels, and pumps, and Section B focusing on valve positions and statuses, together enabling comprehensive understanding of P&ID symbols.

Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify and support the system component descriptions given in Section A, ensuring consistency between textual and graphical information.

Section A references regulatory assumptions and conformance regarding initial core flow and other parameters, while Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section A, indicating that Section A's regulatory analysis is supported by the detailed design documentation in Section B.

Section B provides detailed system evaluation criteria, including thermal efficiency considerations, which should align with the overview values presented in Table 1.3-1 referenced in the analysis question, thus Section B specifies the detailed basis for the overview in Section A.

Section A and Section B both reference data from the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, but Section A discusses system operational procedures while Section B lists regulatory conformance assumptions, with both sections relying on underlying design and operational parameters such as thermal efficiency that are tabulated in Table 1.3-1 and Table 5.1-2.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and thermal margin limits for NuScale's safety analysis, while Section B provides the detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section A.

Section A references regulatory criteria and their applicability to the NuScale design, while Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design information discussed in Section A, connecting general regulatory conformance to specific system representations.

Section B provides detailed system evaluation criteria, including thermal efficiency considerations, which are referenced in Section A's overview of regulatory conformance, connecting the overview and detailed data on NuScale's thermal efficiency.

Section A provides a general overview of the power conversion system, while Section B offers detailed drawings and tables that visually clarify and specify the design information referenced in Section A.

Section B establishes steady state performance criteria, including maximizing electrical output and efficiency, which are quantified in Table 5.1-2, while Section A provides an overview of the power conversion system referenced in Table 1.3-1, thus both sections are connected through the design and evaluation of thermal efficiency values.

Section A provides a general overview of the power conversion system, while Section B offers detailed drawings and tables (such as Table 1.3-1 and Table 5.1-2) that specify the system's operational parameters, including thermal efficiency, thus Section B provides the detailed data that supports or clarifies the overview information in Section A.

Section B provides the detailed evaluation criteria and steady-state parameter values that are used to assess and optimize the performance of the power conversion system described in Section A, specifically including thermal efficiency.

Section A discusses the conformance of the NuScale FSAR with regulatory criteria for ECCS performance and technical specifications, while Section B provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section A, connecting general regulatory requirements to specific design implementations.

Section B provides detailed system evaluation data, including steady state parameters relevant to thermal efficiency, which can be compared to the overview values in Table 1.3-1 referenced in Section A to assess consistency.

Section A and Section B both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and its conformance with regulatory criteria, but Section A focuses on ECCS performance and coolant activity while Section B addresses radiation protection design features, with both sections partially conforming to different regulatory guides as indicated in cross-referenced tables.

Section A discusses conformance with regulatory criteria for coolant system iodine activity, while Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify design information referenced in Section A, connecting regulatory requirements to specific design implementations.

Section B provides detailed system evaluation criteria, including thermal efficiency parameters, which are the basis for the overview values summarized in Table 1.3-1, thus Section B specifies the detailed data that Section A (the overview) summarizes.

Section A and Section B both discuss NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria, but Section A focuses on radiological consequences and coolant iodine activity, while Section B addresses radiation protection design features, with both referencing partial conformance to different regulatory guides.

Section A references and summarizes the regulatory criteria from Section B, specifically noting the applicability and conformance of NuScale's approach to fuel centerline temperatures and uniform cladding strain requirements.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance related to fuel performance criteria, while Section B describes the design and operation of the power conversion system, with both sections connected through the operational parameters (such as heat generation and removal) that impact thermal efficiency.
Section A references and partially conforms to the guidance specified in Section B regarding technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity.
